originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and ice scripts, then initializes many inline crank, piston, valve, pressure, and starter scenes under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, route-local engine model assets, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell, the engine model data, and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the drag, crank, pressure, and starter scenes remain interactive.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with a draggable assembled engine view, then steps through crank, cannon, and simple assembly examples with linked sliders and animated cross-sections.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening drag scene and the early crank and cannon demonstrations under the original published runtime.
notes: The smoke contract treats one opening drag path and one early slider path as required parity behavior.
The route preserves the published progression from simple crank motion into the early combustion and assembly scenes instead of flattening those sections into static diagrams.
evidence: Post-curves rollout planning called for one 3D drag scene and one early assembly or crank slider path.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the opening hero scene responds to drag and the early cannon scene updates from slider input.

originalBehavior: The published article continues through block, crankshaft, pistons, head, valves, camshaft timing, combustion pressure, torque, flywheel, and starter scenes with linked 3D views and synchronized slider updates.
localStatus: The local route keeps the later pressure, torque, camshaft, and starter scenes intact through the original article canvas and slider runtime.
notes: The published 3D engine scenes now load their geometry from the route-local model tree instead of the upstream root path.
The shipped `gears` torque reference is rewritten locally in the public body.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one pressure or torque scene and one later starter or cam-driven scene as parity-critical for the engine batch.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the pressure scene and starter scene update from slider input without runtime asset failures.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with further-watching sections and an upstream footer that includes supporter messaging and author provenance, while also referring back to other Bartosz articles by upstream URL.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published closing sections while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface and rewriting available local cross-links.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The public body no longer includes the Patreon footer prompt or other author-promo surfaces.
evidence: The hardened Ciechanowski audit flags ordinary in-body upstream article links while allowing footer-only provenance.
Route cleanup rewrote the local `gears` torque reference so the family continues converging toward local intra-family navigation.
